title: Tip of the week #1: Project requests and approvals
excerpt:
If you find you’re receiving project requests from multiple sources in various forms (via email, Slack, Jira, etc.), then these can quickly become difficult to track.

posts.author: arubinomunoz (Amalia Rubino @Upscale (part of The Adaptavist Group))
posts.cookedHtml:
If you find you’re receiving project requests from multiple sources in various forms (via email, Slack, Jira, etc.), then these can quickly become difficult to track.

With monday.com, you can standardise your requests and approvals processes using forms. These forms can be shared with internal and external stakeholders, ensuring that all project requests are captured in a consistent format.

By using monday.com to standardise your request and approvals processes, you’ll be able to better manage stakeholder expectations by getting a clearer understanding of inbound requests and how to weigh these up against conflicting requirements and workloads, all visible within one platform.

image1400×810 142 KB

posts.author: FunManAndy (Andy Barker)
posts.cookedHtml:
It’s insane how many companies fall foul of this topic… at a guess, I would say 95% of organisations don’t have a grip on project requests/approvals, and it’s mostly “Whoever shouts the loudest gets the first review/approval”.

So important to ensure standardisation.

And pro-tip if the are more urgent request/escalations:
Have a process for that too!

posts.author: arubinomunoz (Amalia Rubino @Upscale (part of The Adaptavist Group))
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely agree—standardizing project requests and approvals is essential, and having a clear, fair escalation process for urgent needs ensures nothing falls through the cracks!
